qa_pairs.question: How can a user determine if they are requesting a 4-GPU node but only utilizing a single GPU for their task?
qa_pairs.answer: This can be determined by observing the GPU utilization graphs in the portal. For instance, in the 'GPU - Cycle de calcul GPU utilisé' graph, only GPU 0 will show activity (e.g., SM Active, SM Occupancy metrics), while GPUs 1, 2, and 3 will remain inactive with no data recorded. Similar inactivity for the unused GPUs will also be visible in the 'GPU - Power' and 'GPU - Mémoire' graphs.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended approach to optimize resource allocation when a task only utilizes one GPU on a node equipped with four GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: To optimize resource allocation in this situation, users should either adapt their code to effectively utilize all four GPUs available on the node, or, if the task genuinely only requires one GPU, they should adjust their submission script to request only a single GPU by setting `--gpu-per-node=1`.
